hero.title: Hi, I'm <gradient>Bryan Alvarenga</gradient>
hero.text: Web developer passionate about technology, turning ideas into working systems. Backend-focused with PHP, Java and MySQL; experienced with Docker, Bootstrap and Tailwind.

about.title: About
about.sub: I'm a Software Engineering student at Unifio, passionate about building web applications. Strong foundation in Java, PHP, Python, HTML, CSS and JavaScript, with hands-on experience in Tailwind CSS and Bootstrap. Currently learning Spring Boot and Laravel, plus MySQL. I focus on constant improvement through academic and personal projects to build functional, well-structured, impactful solutions.

sge.title: SGE — Athletics Management
sge.desc: PHP, MySQL, Docker, Bootstrap. Event management and public calendar.
sge.coverAlt: Preview of the Athletics Management System

modal.title: SGE — Athletics Management
modal.coverAlt: SGE screen with public calendar and event creation
modal.contextContent: The SGE (Event Management System) is a web application developed for UNIFIO University to modernize and centralize the management of sports and academic events held at the institution's gymnasium. The project was built collaboratively using GitHub version control (branches, pull requests, and code reviews). We also conducted a real requirement-gathering interview with the university coordination to understand their needs.
challengesList: Implement secure authentication with two-factor verification (2FA).
Handle multiple access levels (user, admin, and superadmin).
Build a public calendar view without requiring login.
Maintain asynchronous communication between front-end and back-end using Fetch API and AJAX.
Structure the project under an MVC architecture and integrate PDO, PHPMailer, and Composer.
learningsList: Collaborative development using GitHub branches, commits, PRs, and reviews.
Real experience conducting requirement analysis with institutional clients.
Creation of modular PHP components following MVC best practices.
Secure integration between front-end (Fetch API/AJAX) and PHP back-end.
Containerized setup using Docker for consistent and reproducible environments.

portfolio.title: Personal Portfolio
portfolio.desc: HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript. A showcase to present my work and skills.

modal.title: Personal Portfolio
modal.contextContent: The Personal Portfolio was developed to present my projects, skills, and experience as a web developer. The goal was to create a modern, responsive, and lightweight digital showcase that reflects my personal and professional identity. The project was designed from scratch with a focus on UI/UX details, SEO, and performance, going through multiple iterations to reach the current design.
challengesList: Designing an original layout aligned with my personal brand.
Implementing smooth transitions and animations using IntersectionObserver.
Ensuring full responsiveness across devices and screen sizes.
Maintaining clean and modular code structure (HTML, CSS, and JS separated).
Setting up static deploy with a custom domain and HTTPS via GitHub Pages/Vercel.
learningsList: Responsive design and accessibility best practices.
Performant animations using pure JavaScript.
SEO optimization and metadata structuring.
Continuous deployment and custom domain management.
Building a scalable and easily maintainable structure.

commitlog.title: CommitLog — Technical Blog
commitlog.desc: Personal blog to document learning, projects and architectural decisions during software development.
commitlog.coverAlt: Preview of CommitLog blog showing a list of technical posts.

modal.title: CommitLog — Technical Blog
modal.coverAlt: CommitLog homepage displaying a list of articles
modal.coverCaption: Preview of the blog in production.
modal.contextContent: CommitLog is a personal technical blog created to document learning, projects and reflections about software development. The project was initially designed with a more complex architecture including authentication and database integration, but later evolved into a simpler and more maintainable approach based on MDX and static generation. The main goal is to prioritize writing, clarity and knowledge sharing.
challengesList: Designing a simple architecture focused on content instead of unnecessary complexity.
Building a post system using MDX with static page generation.
Structuring metadata for articles such as slug, tags, date and reading time.
Creating a clean layout focused on readability and navigation between posts.
Organizing the project for easy maintenance and long-term content publishing.
learningsList: Using MDX to combine Markdown with React components.
Implementing static site generation with Next.js.
Organizing blog content using a file-based structure.
Designing minimalist interfaces focused on reading experience.
Understanding the importance of reducing complexity in personal projects.

entrelinhas.title: Entrelinhas — AI-assisted Writing Platform
entrelinhas.desc: Next.js, NestJS, Prisma, PostgreSQL, JWT. AI-powered writing and idea refinement.

modal.title: Entrelinhas — AI-assisted Writing Platform
modal.coverAlt: Entrelinhas interface with text editor and AI-generated suggestions
modal.coverCaption: Application preview with integrated AI agents.
modal.contextContent: Entrelinhas is an experimental application designed to explore new forms of AI-assisted writing. The project was built using a monorepo architecture and leverages AI agents (via Claude Code) capable of interacting directly with the development environment. Its core idea is to create a collaborative workflow where human thinking and AI systems work together to organize, expand, and refine ideas in real time.
challengesList: Integrate AI agents into both the development workflow and the application itself.
Design a scalable architecture using NestJS (backend) and Next.js (frontend).
Implement secure authentication using JWT.
Model flexible and dynamic data structures with Prisma.
Coordinate communication between frontend, backend, and AI services.
learningsList: Practical use of AI agents in real-world software development.
Structuring modern applications with Next.js and NestJS in a monorepo setup.
Implementing authentication with JWT.
Database modeling using Prisma and PostgreSQL.
Product-oriented thinking when designing new writing experiences.
